description: Hot-swap, input protection, configurable UVLO/OVP, inrush control, and optional PMBus telemetry controller for the PDU always-on input branch
notes: Infineon official site confirmed; Digi-Key stock is not required for adoption. Use with external Linear FETs; revalidate voltage rating and settings after the PDU 40V battery x2 combining method and always-on input range are fixed

description: 100V Linear FET for hot-swap, battery protection, and eFuse paths
notes: Infineon official site confirmed; Digi-Key stock is not required for adoption. Back-to-back structure and parallel count depend on input fuse rating, SOA, input voltage range, and inrush current

description: Physical fuse coordinated with the PDU always-on input branch
notes: Coordinate with PDU branch protection, XDP711 current limit, wiring, and connector rating

description: Mechanical and electrical interface from the PDU always-on protected auxiliary bus
notes: Choose a locking connector with vibration retention and current rating matched to the always-on branch

description: 100V synchronous buck controller for 19V/5A design output from the 40V battery x2 always-on branch
notes: Digi-Key active/in-stock; replaces old 55V buck-boost candidate because current requirements are 40V battery x2 always-on input and 19V buck output

description: 100V-class MOSFET pair for 19V synchronous buck power stage
notes: Infineon ISC022N10NM6ATMA1 is active but no Digi-Key stock; choose final FET after input surge limit, thermal design, RDS(on), and gate charge tradeoff
Current rating, saturation current, DCR, size, and temperature rise depend on topology, switching frequency, and ripple target

description: 20mOhm 12V/24V smart high-side switch with protection and diagnosis for Mini PC 19V output
notes: Digi-Key active/in-stock; 5A continuous is thermally meaningful, so verify copper area and derating

description: Cable-mount 5.5mm OD / 2.5mm ID DC plug for center-positive Mini PC 19V output
notes: Use center-positive wiring; board-side cable strain relief or a separate locking board connector is still a mechanical design item

description: 100V 1A synchronous buck regulator for 24V/0.5A passive PoE output from the 40V battery x2 always-on branch
notes: Digi-Key active/in-stock; replaces old 18V-input boost candidate and removes external power MOSFET and rectifier diode

description: Two-channel 12V/24V smart high-side switch with protection and diagnosis for passive PoE 24V output
notes: Digi-Key active/in-stock; use together with a smaller fuse/PTC because the device current limit is higher than 0.5A PoE load current

description: Fuse or resettable PTC for passive PoE output cable protection
notes: Start around 0.75A to 1A class and validate with cable length, load startup, and short-circuit tests

description: Shielded 8P8C RJ45 jack without integrated magnetics for Data In and PoE Out
notes: Use one as Data In and one as PoE Out if the Wi-Fi module uses 24V passive PoE; passive PoE is fixed to 100Mbps operation in this pinout

description: Quad low-capacitance ESD protection diode for RJ45 data pins 1,2,3,6
notes: Protect only the data pins; PoE 24V pins need power TVS/fuse treatment

description: Input TVS, 19V output TVS, 24V output TVS, EMI filters, and shield RC/DNP options
notes: Values depend on cable length, enclosure, PDU high-power noise, and rover shield/GND policy

description: Bulk capacitors, ceramic capacitors, sense resistors, feedback dividers, compensation parts, gate resistors, and pull resistors
notes: Values depend on final 19V and 24V DC/DC topology, controller choice, and layout

description: LEDs and test points for always-on input, 19V, 24V, fault, PMBus/fault pins, and output sense
notes: Keep enough test access for standalone board bring-up without MCU
